extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Two rules share the same trigger 'anticoagulated': rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b:=true, and rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) sets rec_drug_a:=true. The name 'rec_a_from_conjunction' is misleading—its condition is simply 'anticoagulated', not an actual conjunction. With anticoagulated=true, both rules fire: rec_drug_a=true (only rule targeting a) and rec_drug_b=true (only rule targeting b). No higher-priority or competing rules unset them, and defaults only apply to outputs left unset, which is not the case here. The other inputs (g6pd_deficiency, immunosuppressed) only influence rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set to false for simplicity. Thus with anticoagulated=true, g6pd_deficiency=false, immunosuppressed=false, both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are true, making the conjunction true and the mutual-exclusion property false. The property is violated. File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/medication-d1-viol-05.txt
